name: Navarre (Spain)

colors.symbolism: the crimson field of the historic banner of the Kingdom of Navarre
the chains of Navarre and the royal crown
the central emerald of the chains

description: A crimson field bearing in the centre the coat of arms of Navarre - golden chains arranged in a saltire, cross and orle around a central emerald - surmounted by a closed royal crown.
history: The chains of Navarre have been the heraldic symbol of the kingdom since the 13th century, traditionally associated with King Sancho VII's victory at Las Navas de Tolosa in 1212. The flag with the crimson field, chains, and royal crown was officially adopted as the flag of the Chartered Community of Navarre by Foral Law 24/2003 of 4 April.

features.symbolism: the crimson field of the historic banner of the Kingdom of Navarre.

features.role: coat of arms of Navarre, surmounted by a royal crown
features.symbolism: the chains of Navarre, traditionally said to commemorate King Sancho VII the Strong's victory at the Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa (1212) over the Almohad caliph; a central emerald represents the spoils of the battle. The royal crown above marks the historic sovereignty of the Kingdom of Navarre.
